message:
docs(conformance): note DISC-012 fix conflicts with explicit tests (br-tem9 / fd90.79)

Attempted the DISC-012 mixed-tz CSV parse fix described in
fd90.78. Implemented per-row dispatch in
parse_datetime_string_with_timezone_pattern: instead of coercing
opposite-pattern rows to NaT, dispatch to the matching parser
based on the row's own tz_suffix. With this change, the
FP-P2D-429 packet went from FAIL to PASS — confirming the
implementation theory was correct.

However, two existing fp-frame tests explicitly assert the
fail-closed coerce-to-NaT behavior:

  to_datetime_mixed_naive_and_aware_strings_coerces_format_mismatch
  to_datetime_with_options_utc_coerces_mixed_naive_offset_sequence

The test names ("coerces_format_mismatch") show this was a
deliberate design decision, not a missing-feature gap — pandas
parity was traded for fail-closed semantics on mixed inputs.
Flipping the dispatch behavior requires coordinated review with
whoever made that decision: change the tests to reflect the new
behavior, document the resolved discrepancy, etc.

Reverted the implementation change (idle-nudge work shouldn't
override an explicitly-tested intentional design choice without
team review). Updated DISC-012 in DISCREPANCIES.md to note the
conflict and reference the prototype: future contributors who
want to fix this packet now know (a) the fix is straightforward,
(b) it works on the conformance side, (c) it requires updating
the 2 existing tests that document the current fail-closed
behavior.

Tests verified: fp-frame still 1433/1433 passing, fp-io still
429/429 passing after revert. Pure docs change in this commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"frankenpandas-tem9","title":"fd90.79: fix DISC-012 - parse_datetime_string_with_timezone_pattern coerces outliers to NaT","description":"Implement the fix for DISC-012 (refined by fd90.78). parse_datetime_string_with_timezone_pattern currently coerces opposite-pattern rows to NaT, causing parse_csv_datetime_column to bail on mixed-tz CSV columns and leave them as raw input strings. Fix: in the Naive branch, when has_tz_suffix is true, fall through to parse_tz_aware_datetime + format_aware_datetime instead of returning NaT. Same idea (with naive parser fallback) for the Aware branch when has_tz_suffix is false. The infer_mixed_timezone flag's whole point is to support mixed columns, so individual outliers should parse independently while the inferred pattern controls the column-level dtype intent. Test: FP-P2D-429 packet should go from FAIL to PASS after the fix; existing parse_dates tests still green.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:51:21.871924414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:56:00.015040659Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:56:00.012408275Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","fd90","fix","review"]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-thty","title":"[HIGH] style: no rustfmt.toml — style opinions implicit + swarm agents burn cycles on format churn","description":"Style consistency audit.

**Observation.** No `rustfmt.toml` nor `.rustfmt.toml` exists at repo root or in any crate. CI runs `cargo fmt --check` (ci.yml line ~28) which enforces rustfmt's DEFAULT style — but the workspace has not captured any style-choice decisions.

**Consequence in an active multi-agent swarm.**
- Each agent's local rustfmt may differ from CI's if they use different nightly dates (see br-1d9y about nightly pin). A nightly-to-nightly rustfmt diff can reformat hundreds of lines silently, breaking `git diff` review for the PR.
- Opinions like `max_width = 100` (default) vs 120 (often preferred for wide dataframe column lists), `imports_granularity = "Crate"` vs "Module", `group_imports = "StdExternalCrate"`, `edition = "2024"` (important for the workspace's 2024-edition choice!) — all live in rustfmt.toml and all are currently unfixed, defaulting silently.
- Today's session: my edits to fp-frame/src/lib.rs (row_multiindex field, group_keys_as_row_multiindex helper) had my rustfmt reformat the surrounding `.groupby(&groupby_refs)` from one line to three (commit f7a4e59, original lib-updater noted the reformatting as incidental). With no rustfmt.toml, this is agent-specific churn, not a shared style decision.

**Secondary: `edition = "2024"` in per-crate Cargo.toml files is NOT sufficient for rustfmt** — rustfmt.toml has its own `edition = "2024"` field that the workspace currently doesn't set. Mismatch between Cargo.toml edition and rustfmt edition produces incorrect formatting in rare cases (2024-specific syntax like the `gen` keyword).

**Severity.** HIGH — compound cost across every PR. Each swarm agent burns cycles on style churn rather than code review. CI catches the final state but doesn't prevent the churn.

**Fix shape:**

`rustfmt.toml` at repo root, conservative but explicit:
```
edition = "2024"
max_width = 100
hard_tabs = false
tab_spaces = 4
newline_style = "Unix"
use_small_heuristics = "Default"
imports_granularity = "Crate"
group_imports = "StdExternalCrate"
reorder_imports = true
reorder_modules = true
unstable_features = true    # rustfmt grew many opinions behind nightly
```

(The project is already pinned to nightly via rust-toolchain.toml, so unstable rustfmt options are available.)

Also:
1. ci.yml's `cargo fmt --check` already works but gains enforcement of the explicit opinions once rustfmt.toml lands.
2. Pre-commit hook: add `.git/hooks/hooks.d/pre-commit/10-rustfmt.sh` that runs `cargo fmt -- --check` (or `rustfmt --check` on staged .rs files) before commit. Fails fast locally, saves a CI round-trip.
3. Document the style choice in CONTRIBUTING.md (sibling br-frankenpandas-6d5s).

Blast radius: 1 new file (~15 lines), 1 new pre-commit hook (~20 lines), possibly a one-time reformatting commit after rustfmt.toml lands (large diff but mechanical). Recommend batching the reformat into a single commit tagged `chore: apply rustfmt.toml opinions` that reviewers can fast-scroll past.

Related:
- br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) — style notes live here.
- br-1d9y (nightly date pin) — rustfmt version implicitly pinned by the nightly pin, reducing reformat drift.
- br-iy8u (pre-commit doctrine) — adding a rustfmt hook extends the existing pre-commit discipline.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:39:17.551317919Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:28:26.542652618Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:28:26.540109900Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review","rustfmt","style","swarm"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-tihp","title":"fp-columnar Column.sort_values argsort parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:46:53.631944796Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:11:48.184280799Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:11:48.182540153Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of frankenpandas-a1d1/q11d (sort_values+argsort); already shipped in commit 3f490e1","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -81,7 +81,7 @@
 - **Reference:** Pandas handles a CSV column with mixed naive + tz-aware datetime strings by parsing each row independently (the naive rows produce `Timestamp` without tz; the aware rows produce `Timestamp` with tz). When converted to strings, both forms are reformatted into pandas' canonical `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS[±HH:MM]` shape.
 - **Our impl:** fp-frame's `to_datetime_with_options(infer_mixed_timezone=true)` infers ONE tz pattern (Naive or Aware) from the FIRST non-null row. Rows that don't match that pattern are coerced to `NaT`. fp-io's `parse_csv_datetime_column` then sees the partial-parse failure and returns `None`, leaving the entire column as the raw input strings. So the second row in a mixed-tz column keeps its original `2024-01-15T10:30:00Z` form even though our `format_aware_datetime` would have rendered it correctly as `2024-01-15 10:30:00+00:00`.
 - **Impact:** Conformance packet `FP-P2D-429` (`csv_read_frame_parse_dates_mixed_timezone_strict`) fails: `actual=Utf8("2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"), expected=Utf8("2024-01-15 10:30:00+00:00")`. The actual form is the unmodified CSV input; the expected form is what pandas would emit after parsing.
-- **Resolution:** ACCEPTED for now — fix requires `to_datetime_with_options` to parse each row independently when input is mixed (not pick a single inferred pattern), and `parse_csv_datetime_column` to keep partial successes instead of bailing out. Tracked as a future fp-frame slice. Per br-frankenpandas-xp63 (fd90.77) / br-frankenpandas-gsrv (fd90.78).
+- **Resolution:** ACCEPTED for now — fix requires `to_datetime_with_options` to parse each row independently when input is mixed (not pick a single inferred pattern), and `parse_csv_datetime_column` to keep partial successes instead of bailing out. NB: the current coerce-to-NaT behavior is **explicitly documented** by tests `to_datetime_mixed_naive_and_aware_strings_coerces_format_mismatch` and `to_datetime_with_options_utc_coerces_mixed_naive_offset_sequence` in fp-frame, so flipping the per-row dispatch needs coordinated review (those test assertions need to update too). br-frankenpandas-tem9 (fd90.79) prototyped the per-row dispatch fix and confirmed it makes FP-P2D-429 green but breaks the 2 fail-closed tests above — reverted pending design decision. Tracked as a future fp-frame slice. Per br-frankenpandas-xp63 (fd90.77) / br-frankenpandas-gsrv (fd90.78) / br-frankenpandas-tem9 (fd90.79, reverted).
 - **Tests affected:** `packet_filter_runs_csv_read_frame_parse_dates_mixed_timezone_packet`.
 - **Review date:** 2026-04-26
